name: Salpornithidae
description: Spotted Creepers are a small family of tree-creeping passerine birds found in Africa and Asia. Unlike true treecreepers, they have spotted and barred plumage and frequent open woodlands rather than dense forests.

children.name: Salpornis
children.description: A genus of bark-gleaning passerines with heavily spotted and barred plumage, found in Africa and India.

children.name: Salpornis spilonotus
children.description: The Indian Spotted Creeper is a small, brownish bird heavily spotted and barred with white on the upperparts. It creeps along tree trunks and branches in dry deciduous forests of India and Nepal.

children.name: Salpornis salvadori
children.description: The African Spotted Creeper is a small, cryptically patterned bird with spotted and barred brown plumage. It is found in miombo and savanna woodlands across sub-Saharan Africa, foraging on tree bark for insects.
